sprint: Traveller Gateway -- Creator Signature Decline Becomes a Real Choice (Retry / Continue as a Traveller)
task: "the sequence is good. only step 5 option b" -> (a clarifying AskUserQuestion attempt was explicitly rejected by the user) -> "did u got the ans ?" -> "retry or continue as traveller . options". Step 5 is the Returning Creator path's Creator Signature tap-grid check (js/magicCardUI.js's MagicCardUI.beginCreatorSignature, called from js/gatewaySequence.js's runVideoSequence). Its existing retry-in-place behaviour on a wrong tap was already correct and untouched -- the real gap was the challenge's own '<- Back' escape hatch, which previously called onResult(false,null) immediately and silently, clearing the active Magic Card identity and falling straight through into beginSegment1() using the SAME opts object still bound to the Returning Creator's own no-egg video and reunion-toned LUMO_ARRIVAL_RETURNING_LINES -- so declining showed a cleared identity riding along on a video/greeting that still said 'It's good to see you again,' an internally inconsistent, previously-silent bug. Fixed in two places. (1) beginCreatorSignature is rewritten around two named inner functions, showChallenge()/showChoice(): the challenge's onBack now opens a small, real choice screen instead of deciding anything on its own -- 'Not able to show your stars right now?' followed by a bold primary '\ud83d\udd01 Try Again' button (re-opens a fresh showChallenge()) and a quiet '\ud83c\udf31 Continue as a Traveller' link (the only path that now calls onResult(false,null)) -- both reusing 100% pre-existing CSS classes (.magic-card-gate-continue, .magic-card-gate-notyou, .magic-card-gate-welcome) and the file's own _el() helper, zero new CSS needed. (2) runVideoSequence's decline branch in js/gatewaySequence.js no longer just clears the identity and continues down the Returning Creator's own path -- since verification always happens BEFORE Segment 1 ever plays, the mounted no-egg video is still paused on its very first (closed-doors) frame at this exact moment, so the fix cleanly tears down that unplayed video/wrap, mounts the real Traveller clip (GATE_FINAL_VIDEO_SRC/GATE_FINAL_POSTER_SRC, WITH the Story Egg), and recursively re-invokes the same runVideoSequence(video,opts) mechanism with the Traveller's own opts (preLines:null, pauseLines:GREETING_LINES) -- a declined check now genuinely BECOMES the real Traveller experience (its own clip, its own first-meeting 5-line greeting), not a Returning-Creator-flavoured one with a quietly-cleared identity underneath it. Verified via a new, dedicated 11-assertion Playwright suite covering all three real paths end-to-end: Scenario A confirms '<- Back' opens the real choice screen (correct button text, tap-grid genuinely gone underneath, not just covered); Scenario B confirms 'Try Again' re-opens a genuinely fresh challenge and a correct pattern tap afterward still succeeds normally; Scenario C confirms 'Continue as a Traveller' swaps the mounted video to the real gate-sequence-final.mp4 (confirmed via URL string check excluding 'no-egg'), clears the active Magic Card id from localStorage, and shows the Traveller's own 'Welcome, Traveller.' greeting (not the reunion pair) at the pause point, with zero unexpected console errors. Full regression across the entire existing Gateway/Magic-Card/Creator Playwright suite (gw_final_video_test.js 15/15, gw_returning_unified_test.js 18/18, gw_completion_check.js, gateway_skip_check.js, gw_myprojects_clear_check.js 5/5, gw_smoke_handoff.js 6/6) and both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2, unaffected -- this fix touches zero Builder files) pass unchanged, zero console errors. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; no CSS changes were needed, so no cache-bust bump was required this time; confined to js/gatewaySequence.js and js/magicCardUI.js.
